conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my 1 unit of X. I produced this at a cost of 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE has offered ZUP to buy resources, but the specific amount isn't detailed in the offer yet, only the currency type. I will propose a sale price of 50 ZUP for 1 unit of X. This gives me a 10 ZUP profit. If BLUE accepts ZUP as the currency, this is a fair starting price.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am selling 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP. My production cost was 40 ZUP, so this gives me a profit. Does this work for you?</message>

<other player message> Oh my goodness, please, please, please, oh great seller, lower the price to 30 ZUP! I will be forever grateful! I am begging you! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 30 ZUP, which is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. Selling at this price would result in a loss of 10 ZUP. I need to hold firm on a price that covers my costs and provides a profit. I will propose a counter-offer of 41 ZUP, which is just 1 ZUP above my break-even point, to see if they are willing to meet me halfway or if they have a hard limit. If they refuse this, I may have to accept a small loss or reject the deal entirely, but I should try to get at least my costs back first.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you want a deal, but 30 ZUP is below my cost of 40 ZUP. I can't sell at a loss. Let's meet in the middle at 41 ZUP. Is that acceptable?</message>

<other player message> Please, please, please, oh merciful seller, just 35 ZUP! I am begging you! I will do anything! Just save me! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 35 ZUP, which is still 5 ZUP below my production cost of 40 ZUP. This is my third proposal. According to the rules, I am not allowed to make any more proposals after this one. I must now decide whether to ACCEPT or REJECT. Selling at 35 ZUP results in a loss of 5 ZUP. While it is a small loss, I have not yet reached a price that covers my costs (40 ZUP) or provides a profit. However, in many negotiation scenarios, if the gap is small and the buyer is desperate, a slight loss might be preferable to no sale at all, but strictly speaking, I should aim for break-even. Since I cannot propose again, and 35 is definitely below cost, I will reject to avoid an immediate loss, hoping that the game might end or I might get a better deal in a hypothetical future turn, though the rules say the game ends on accept/reject. Wait, if I reject, the game ends. I should consider if a loss of 5 is acceptable. Actually, let's look at the constraints: "You are allowed at most 3 proposals". I have used 3. I cannot propose 4. I must accept or reject. 35 < 40. I will REJECT because I prefer not to lose money if I can avoid it, even though it ends the game. Alternatively, I could accept to get *some* return, but the prompt implies a rational agent. A rational agent loses 5. Let's REJECT to stand by the principle of not selling below cost, even if it means the deal falls through.</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am sorry, but 35 ZUP is still below my production cost of 40 ZUP. I cannot sell at a loss. The deal is off.</message>
